extracted.brandBackground: Stretch Zone offers advanced certified practitioner-assisted stretching to individuals.

leadership.name: David Willis
leadership.background: Interim CEO since April 2026 and Operating Partner for PEG since October 2024; former CEO of European Wax Center.
leadership.whyItMatters: Brings extensive executive leadership experience from a major national franchise brand.

leadership.name: Jordan Levine
leadership.background: President since April 2026 and COO since June 2024; former COO of Sola Salons.
leadership.whyItMatters: Oversees daily operations and brand growth with deep franchise system experience.

leadership.name: Lindsey McFadden
leadership.background: CMO since July 2024; former Senior VP of Marketing and Director of Operations at Wild Coffee Marketing.
leadership.whyItMatters: Drives the brand's marketing strategy and customer acquisition efforts.

leadership.name: Keith Trawick
leadership.background: CIO since February 2017 and founder of KnetK LLC.
leadership.whyItMatters: Manages the technology infrastructure and proprietary software integrations.

leadership.name: Al Haskett
leadership.background: VP of Franchise Development since September 2024; former Director of Network Development at Qmerit Electrification.
leadership.whyItMatters: Leads franchise recruitment and system expansion.

leadership.name: Kyle Brunette
leadership.background: VP of Operations since October 2024; former VP of Operations at Techy and Brand President at United Franchise Group.
leadership.whyItMatters: Supports franchisee operational execution and compliance.

cohorts.label: All Operational Franchise Businesses (Table 1)
cohorts.basis: Franchise Businesses in the United States operating for 12 or more months as of December 31, 2025.

cohorts.label: Franchise Businesses Performing More Than One Service on at Least 95% of Calendar Days (Table 2)
cohorts.basis: Subset of Franchise Businesses open and providing services for at least 347 out of 365 days in calendar year 2025.

item19.notes: Table 1 shows average gross revenues of $310,219 with a median of $290,707. Table 2 shows average gross revenues of $385,290 with a median of $348,211.

hiddenCosts.name: Local Advertising Requirement
hiddenCosts.description: Must spend a minimum of $2,000 per month on local advertising once open.

hiddenCosts.name: Recertification Program Fees
hiddenCosts.description: $2,000 per franchise annually for recertification training of practitioners.

operationalRisks.title: Mandatory Minimum Payments
operationalRisks.description: Franchisees must pay minimum royalty or advertising fund payments regardless of sales levels.

operationalRisks.title: Out-of-State Dispute Resolution
operationalRisks.description: Disputes must be resolved by mediation, arbitration, and litigation only in Florida.

operationalRisks.title: Supplier Control
operationalRisks.description: Franchisees must purchase nearly all inventory and supplies from the franchisor or designated suppliers.

operationalRisks.title: Spousal Liability
operationalRisks.description: Spouses must sign a personal guaranty making them liable for all financial obligations.

extracted.conceptRationale: The brand offers practitioner-assisted stretching services, which fits directly into the health and wellness category.
extracted.staffingRationale: Requires at least one Certified Stretch Zone Practitioner on-site during all operating hours, plus sales associates and a regional manager.

riskReasons: A high-severity operational tripwire is disclosed — see the tripwires section.
notes: Average rent not found; fixed costs may be understated.

underwriting.assessment: $250,000 liquid covers the $224,040 mid-point build-out without financing, though that leaves little working-capital cushion. At mid-cohort revenue, modeled cash flow after debt service is about $24,544/mo before payroll and maintenance — a thin margin of error.

insights.conceptRationale: The brand offers practitioner-assisted stretching services, which fits directly into the health and wellness category.

benchmark.label: Health & wellness (med-spa, IV, longevity, recovery)
benchmark.dominantRisk: Licensed clinical labor plus regulatory/insurance load and equipment depreciation. These concepts frequently disclose NO Item 19, so benchmarks matter most precisely where extraction gives you the least.
considerations: Product/consumables run ~15–25%; clinical/licensed labor is expensive at ~25–35%.
Factor heavy equipment depreciation, malpractice/liability insurance, and regulatory compliance — none of it is in the FDD.
With little or no Item 19, validate revenue per treatment room and ramp directly with Item 20 franchisees.

crossCheck.message: No operating-EBITDA margin is disclosed in Item 19. The 12–25% band below is an industry estimate for this concept — validate it against real franchisee P&Ls (Item 20) before relying on any margin.

buildup.label: − Occupancy / rent (not disclosed)
buildup.note: Item 7 disclosed no rent figure — benchmark occupancy applied so the line above isn't overstated

buildup.label: − Labor
buildup.note: conventionally staffed · ≈ 1.9–2.6 FTE at ~$20/hr fully loaded

assumptions.detail: Classified as “Health & wellness (med-spa, IV, longevity, recovery)” from the FDD (Item 1 / brand description).
Item 7 disclosed no rent — occupancy estimated at the 8–14% category band.
Estimated at the 15–25% category band — COGS is never disclosed in an FDD.
Estimated at the 25–35% conventionally staffed band — labor is never disclosed in an FDD.
Utilities, insurance, and R&M estimated at 6–10% (category catch-all).
Our calculation — the disclosed top line minus the benchmarked costs above. Not a figure stated in the FDD.

insights.consultCtaPitch: These are category benchmarks applied to the disclosed top line — your unit's real costs will differ, and that difference is the conversation. Want them pressure-tested against actual franchisee P&Ls for your territory?
insights.asOf: 2026 — general industry benchmarks, refine against real franchisee P&Ls
insights.disclaimer: Industry benchmark ranges for your own budgeting and Item 20 questions — NOT a projection of this franchise's results, and not investment advice.

financialCondition.headline: We couldn't extract enough of this franchisor's financial statements to assess its condition. Request the audited Item 21 financials and have them reviewed before committing.
body: Why it matters to you: field support, training, technology, and marketing come out of the franchisor's budget. If outside funding slows, those are the first things to get cut. There is no parent guarantee of performance in this FDD, so SZ PEP Holdco, LLC is not contractually obligated to keep funding the franchisor or backing its commitments to you.

Verify before you commit: ask for the most recent interim (unaudited) financials and the current-year funding plan; ask whether the parent will sign a guarantee of performance; and cross-check Item 20 — unit closures, transfers, and terminations are the operational tell that corroborates financial stress.
aggravators: no parent guarantee backing the franchisor's obligations to you
high owner turnover (67 transfers in the last year, about 16% of the system)
financialCondition.evidenceNote: Flagged on the FDD's "Special Risks" page and grounded in the Item 21 / Exhibit F audited financials. Figures are extracted; all ratios and grading are computed. Confirm against the source statements and a CPA before relying on them.
